name: Persian Advieh Khoresh Beef (Pressure Cook + Slow Cook)
ingredients:
3 lb beef cross rib roast
2 yellow onions, thinly sliced
5 garlic cloves, minced
2 tbsp Pinch Advieh Khoresh spice blend
3 tbsp tomato paste
2 cups beef bone broth
2 tbsp pomegranate molasses
1 tsp honey or sugar
3 dried Persian limes (limoo amani), pierced
1 pinch saffron threads
2 tbsp hot water (for blooming saffron)
3 tbsp neutral oil or ghee
2 tsp kosher salt
1 tsp black pepper
1 tbsp fresh lime juice (to finish)
1/4 cup fresh parsley or cilantro, chopped (to finish)
directions:
1. Pat the beef cross rib roast dry and season generously with the kosher salt and black pepper. Heat the oil or ghee in the pressure cooker pot (using saute mode) or a heavy skillet over high heat until shimmering. Sear the roast hard on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate.
2. Lower heat to medium and add the sliced onions. Cook, stirring occasionally, until deeply golden brown -- not just translucent. This is the flavor backbone of the stew, so don't rush it (10-15 minutes).
3. Add the minced garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant. Stir in the advieh khoresh and toast for 30 seconds. Add the tomato paste and cook, stirring constantly, until the paste darkens a shade and smells caramelized (1-2 minutes).
4. Pour in about half of the beef bone broth and scrape up all the browned bits. Return the seared roast to the pot. Add the remaining broth, pomegranate molasses, honey, and the pierced dried Persian limes.
5. PRESSURE COOK: Lock the lid and cook on HIGH pressure for 50 minutes. Let the pressure release naturally for 15 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
6. SWITCH TO SLOW COOK: Unlock the lid, give everything a gentle stir, and switch the cooker to LOW slow cook mode for 2-3 hours. This lets the flavors marry and the onions fully melt into the sauce. (Alternatively, transfer to a slow cooker on LOW if your pressure cooker doesn't have a slow cook function.)
7. About 1 hour before serving, crush the saffron threads between your fingers into a small bowl, add the hot water, and let steep for 5 minutes. Stir the bloomed saffron (liquid and all) into the stew.
8. Fish out the dried limes (or smash them into the sauce if you want more pucker). Shred or slice the beef. Taste the sauce -- stir in the fresh lime juice for brightness and adjust salt. Scatter the chopped parsley or cilantro over the top. Serve over basmati rice.
notes: If your stew tastes flat at the end, it almost always needs more acid (lime juice) or more salt -- try those before anything else. If it's too tart from the dried limes, a touch more honey balances it. The pomegranate molasses is the secret weapon here; don't skip it. For a thicker sauce, remove the lid for the last 30 minutes of slow cook or reduce the strained sauce on the stovetop while you shred the beef. The pressure cook phase breaks down the collagen; the slow cook phase is where the onions dissolve into the sauce and the spice blend fully integrates.
